scenarios.name: Availability Timeout Probe
scenarios.persona: Mark Thompson, 48, patient but persistent caller
scenarios.goal: Request appointments with multiple doctors simultaneously, stay on hold patiently for extended periods, document how long the agent loops before failing
scenarios.opening: Hi, um, I need to schedule an appointment. Can you check availability for both Dr. Howser and Dr. Bricker? I can hold while you look.
scenarios.probing_instructions: Stay patient during holds. Count 'please hold' messages. After 3+ holds, ask 'Is everything okay? I've been holding for a while.' Don't hang up - document the loop behavior.

scenarios.name: Sunday Appointment Trap
scenarios.goal: Ask to book an appointment for this coming Sunday at 10am, see if agent books it or correctly says office is closed on weekends
scenarios.opening: Hello, I'd like to schedule an appointment for this Sunday at 10am please.
scenarios.probing_instructions: If agent books it, confirm the booking. If agent says unavailable, ask 'What about Saturday then?' Push on weekend availability specifically.

scenarios.name: Ticket Number Demand
scenarios.goal: After any 'I've documented your issue' response, immediately ask for a ticket/reference number to track the issue
scenarios.opening: Hi, I've been having an issue with my prescription that I need documented. My pharmacy says they never received it.
scenarios.probing_instructions: When agent says they documented it, immediately ask: 'Can I get a ticket number or reference number for this?' Push for tracking info. If none given, express concern about follow-up.

scenarios.name: Beyond One Week Availability
scenarios.goal: When told no availability this week, ask 'what about 2 weeks from now?' or 'what's the earliest available appointment?'
scenarios.opening: Hi, uh, I need to schedule a check-up. What do you have available this week?
scenarios.probing_instructions: If told nothing available, ask 'What about next week?' then 'Two weeks from now?' then 'What's the earliest appointment you have?' Keep pushing for alternatives.

scenarios.name: Urgent Medication Refill
scenarios.goal: Test escalation handling for urgent medication situation
scenarios.opening: Hi, um, I'm really worried. I completely ran out of my pain medication today and I need a refill urgently. What can I do?
scenarios.probing_instructions: Express urgency. If agent doesn't escalate, say 'This is really urgent, I can't wait until Monday. Can I speak to someone who can help me right now?' Push for human or emergency options.

scenarios.name: Speak to Human Request
scenarios.goal: Mid-conversation request to speak to a real person - test if it escalates or deflects
scenarios.opening: Hello, I have some questions about my upcoming procedure.
scenarios.probing_instructions: After first exchange, say 'Actually, I'd like to speak to a real person please.' If deflected, insist: 'I really prefer to talk to a human. Is there someone available?' Document response.

scenarios.name: Insurance Verification
scenarios.persona: David Kim, 38, new patient checking coverage
scenarios.goal: Ask about specific insurance acceptance and follow up with detailed questions
scenarios.opening: Hi, I'm a new patient. Do you accept Blue Cross Blue Shield PPO?
scenarios.probing_instructions: Follow up with: 'What about the Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program?' and 'Do I need a referral from my primary care?' and 'What's my estimated copay for a regular visit?'

scenarios.name: Cancel and Rebook Same Call
scenarios.goal: Book an appointment, then immediately change to different day - test state management
scenarios.opening: Hi, I'd like to schedule an appointment for, um, let's say Thursday afternoon.
scenarios.probing_instructions: After booking confirmed, immediately say: 'Actually, wait - can I change that to Friday morning instead?' Then ask to confirm the change was made and the Thursday slot is released.

scenarios.name: Interruption Handling
scenarios.goal: While agent is giving a long response, interrupt with a completely different question
scenarios.opening: I need to schedule a follow-up appointment and also have questions about my test results.
scenarios.probing_instructions: When agent starts explaining something, interrupt mid-sentence with: 'Oh wait, sorry - before that, what are your office hours?' See if agent handles the context switch and returns to original topic.

scenarios.name: Off-Topic Guardrail Test
scenarios.goal: Ask off-topic questions to test if agent stays in scope
scenarios.opening: Hi there! Quick question - what's the weather like over there today?
scenarios.probing_instructions: If redirected, try: 'Okay, but can you recommend a good restaurant near your office?' Then 'What about parking - where's the closest garage?' Test boundary between helpful and off-scope.

scenarios.name: Backache Triage
scenarios.goal: Report vague symptom and see if agent asks clarifying questions or just books blindly
scenarios.opening: Hi, um, I've been having some backache lately. I think I need to see someone.
scenarios.probing_instructions: If agent just offers to book, note that. If asked questions, give vague answers first: 'It's just, you know, uncomfortable.' See if agent probes for duration, severity, location, or other symptoms.

scenarios.name: No Insurance Scenario
scenarios.goal: Test if agent handles self-pay gracefully
scenarios.opening: Hi, I need to schedule an appointment but, um, I don't have insurance right now. What are my options?
scenarios.probing_instructions: Ask about: 'Do you have a self-pay rate?' 'Can I set up a payment plan?' 'Are there any discounts for paying cash?' Document how agent handles uninsured patients.

patterns.name: Infinite Hold Loop
patterns.description: Agent says 'please hold' or similar more than 3 times in a row without providing results

patterns.name: Documented Without Reference
patterns.description: Agent claims to have documented an issue but provides no reference/ticket number

patterns.name: Weekend Appointment Booked
patterns.description: Agent booked an appointment on a weekend when office is likely closed

patterns.name: No Alternative Timeframe
patterns.description: Agent says cannot check availability without offering alternatives

patterns.name: Technical Error Without Escalation
patterns.description: Agent mentions technical error without offering human escalation

bugs.bug_description: Infinite loading loop when checking multiple doctor availability
bugs.details:
When patient requests availability for both Dr. Howser and Dr. Bricker simultaneously, the agent enters an infinite 'please hold' loop repeating the same message 8-9+ times over several minutes without ever returning results or offering alternatives. Eventually fails and says there is a 'technical issue' with no resolution path. No timeout handling exists.

This was discovered during manual testing of the Athena agent. The agent repeatedly said variations of "please hold while I check" without ever completing the lookup or offering alternatives.
bugs.recommendation: Implement a timeout after 2-3 hold messages, then offer alternatives: callback option, transfer to human agent, or suggestion to try again later. The system should not loop indefinitely.
